ReleaseLabel: dabbler-ai-orchestration 0.41.0 local VSIX (Set 087 'Module-Organized Projects - Foundations', Session 3 of 4; the version bump happens at release, after Session 4)
DocumentDate: 7/10/2026 (revised same day after operator feedback: walks now follow the form's natural top-to-bottom order, and every intentionally skipped step is called out)
Notes:
Ad-hoc per-session UAT for Session 3: the 'New module' scaffold (a docs/modules.yaml entry + a plan stub), module-targeted plan authoring / decomposition (the flows ask WHICH module and stamp 'module: <slug>' into generated specs), and the CODEOWNERS + monorepo-CI teaching templates new projects now scaffold with. Sessions 1-2 (the manifest reader and the Explorer's module grouping) were verified earlier under the operator-armed E2E gate and are NOT re-walked here.

=== ORDER AND FOLDERS - READ THIS FIRST ===
You will use THREE fresh empty folders. Run the walks strictly in numbered order.
  Folder A ('uat-modules', e.g. C:\temp\uat-modules): walks 1-6, following the Getting Started form top-to-bottom - walk 1 is the form's step 1 (Build project structure), walks 2-4 use step 2's New module button and the Command Palette, walk 5 uses step 2's planning-prompt button, walk 6 uses step 3's decomposition button. Nothing is skipped in folder A.
  Folder B ('uat-single', e.g. C:\temp\uat-single): walk 7 only. You will deliberately NOT run Build project structure in this folder - the walk says so again when you get there.
  Folder C ('uat-bare', e.g. C:\temp\uat-bare): walk 8 only. You will deliberately NOT build anything and NOT create any module - the walk says so again when you get there.
Each walk begins by telling you which folder you are in and what state it should be in, so you never have to guess whether skipping something is expected.
===========================================

Every step below was ALREADY exercised for real before this checklist was offered to you: the full extension unit suite (1,353 passing / 0 failing, incl. 40 new Set-087-S3 tests) plus a live end-to-end dogfood run on 2026-07-10 that drove the real new-module flow, the real module picker, and the real prompt builders against a scratch repo (12/12 PASS; the exact strings quoted in each Expectation are from that run and from current source, including the verification round 1-2 fixes). What automation genuinely cannot check - and what this walk is FOR - is the real VS Code UI surface: input boxes appearing with the right copy, the QuickPick, toasts, and the buttons in the Getting Started form. UAT runs against a LOCALLY BUILT VSIX ONLY: the orchestrator built one for you during the session at tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.41.0.vsix (it contains the Set 087 S1+S2+S3 changes INCLUDING both verification-round fixes). Install it via Extensions > '...' menu > 'Install from VSIX...', then reload when prompted. Walk 1 chooses a $0 budget with 'Skip verification' ON PURPOSE so the walk spends nothing.

Review.Subarea: Walk 1 - form step 1: Build project structure (includes the new .github templates)
Review.ItemLabel: Building the structure works as before AND now writes the CODEOWNERS + monorepo-CI teaching templates
Review.HumanAction:
WHERE YOU ARE: Folder A does not exist yet - you create it in this walk. Nothing is skipped in this walk; you are doing the form's step 1 first, exactly as the form presents it.
1. Create a new EMPTY folder C:\temp\uat-modules and open it in VS Code (File > Open Folder...).
2. Click the Dabbler AI Orchestration icon in the Activity Bar. The Getting Started form appears with steps 1-2-3.
3. In step '1. Build project structure', leave the 'Full' radio and 'Direct provider API keys' selected. In 'Verification budget (USD, not-to-exceed)' type exactly: 0
4. In the choice that appears below the budget box, select 'Skip verification' (deliberate - this walk must spend nothing).
5. Click 'Build project structure' and wait for it to finish (the venv install can take a minute or two).
6. When the summary toast appears, open the file tree (Explorer view) and expand the new .github folder.
Review.Expectation: The toast begins 'Project structure built (full tier):' and counts the files written. Step 1 in the form now shows a green check. The folder contains .github/CODEOWNERS and .github/workflows/monorepo-ci.yml (both NEW in this release). CODEOWNERS is ENTIRELY comment lines (every non-blank line starts with '#') and includes the worked Priya/Sam/Alex example plus a comment block explaining the integration `touches` review rule. monorepo-ci.yml declares 'name: Monorepo CI', triggers on push to main + pull_request, and its only ACTIVE job is 'all-modules' whose step is an echo placeholder that says to replace it - committing these files as-is cannot break a build or assign a reviewer.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedActions.test.ts > 'writes exactly the structure artifacts and NO starter session set' (asserts both .github paths); gitScaffoldCore.test.ts full-render tests; coldStartSnapshot.test.ts golden includes both files for both tiers; the monorepo-ci template is valid YAML (loaded + parsed by the bundle tests).

Review.Subarea: Walk 2 - form step 2: the New module button
Review.ItemLabel: The step-2 'New module...' button scaffolds a manifest entry + plan stub and opens the stub
Review.HumanAction:
WHERE YOU ARE: still in Folder A (uat-modules). Walk 1 is done: step 1 shows a green check, and the form is still on screen because no session sets exist yet. NOTE: this button does NOT require walk 1 - it works even in a bare folder - but in this checklist you built first, so nothing is out of order.
1. In step '2. Create or import a project plan', find the button labeled exactly 'New module...' (it is the third button, after 'Copy prompt for planning'). Hover it for ~2 seconds and read the tooltip.
2. Click 'New module...'. An input box titled 'New module (1/2): slug' appears with placeholder 'greeter'. Type exactly: greeter and press Enter.
3. A second input box titled 'New module (2/2): display title' appears. Type exactly: Greeter and press Enter.
Review.Expectation: The hover tooltip in step 1 begins 'Optional, for teams: declare a module in docs/modules.yaml'. After step 3: (a) an editor tab opens on docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md whose first line is exactly '# Greeter — module project plan'; (b) a toast reads exactly: Module "greeter" declared in a new docs/modules.yaml. Plan stub created at docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md — fill it in, then decompose it into session sets. (c) the file docs/modules.yaml exists and starts with a comment block headed '# docs/modules.yaml — the module manifest' followed by 'modules:' and one entry with 'slug: greeter', 'title: "Greeter"', 'codeRoots: []' and 'planPath: docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md'.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: moduleAuthoring.test.ts > 'runNewModuleFlow' happy-path + 'scaffoldNewModule' fresh-manifest tests (real fs); AI exploratory check 2026-07-10: drove the real flow against a scratch repo (Walk A of the dogfood run, PASS). gettingStartedHtml.test.ts pins the button + hover copy.

Review.Subarea: Walk 3 - slug validation is live and fail-loud
Review.ItemLabel: Bad slugs are rejected inline with specific messages; Esc writes nothing
Review.HumanAction:
WHERE YOU ARE: still in Folder A. The greeter module from walk 2 exists.
1. Click 'New module...' again. In the slug box type exactly: Greeter (capital G) and read the inline validation message under the box.
2. Clear it and type exactly: greeter (the module you just made) and read the message.
3. Press Esc to cancel.
4. Open docs/modules.yaml and count the entries.
Review.Expectation: Step 1 shows exactly: Module slugs are kebab-case: lowercase letters and digits, joined by single hyphens (e.g. "greeter", "payment-api"). Step 2 shows exactly: Module "greeter" already exists in docs/modules.yaml. While either message shows, Enter does not accept the value. After Esc (step 3), docs/modules.yaml still has exactly ONE entry (greeter) - nothing was written.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: moduleAuthoring.test.ts > 'slug validation' matrix (exact messages) + 'Esc on either input box cancels without writing'.

Review.Subarea: Walk 4 - the Command Palette path (second module)
Review.ItemLabel: 'Dabbler: New Module' appends to the existing manifest without touching its text
Review.HumanAction:
WHERE YOU ARE: still in Folder A. This walk uses the Command Palette instead of the form button - both run the SAME flow; the palette is the path you will use later in real repos, where the Getting Started form is no longer shown.
1. Open docs/modules.yaml and add a comment line anywhere above the 'modules:' line, e.g.: # my note. Save the file.
2. Ctrl+Shift+P > type 'Dabbler: New Module' > Enter.
3. Slug: type exactly clock and press Enter. Title: press Enter WITHOUT typing anything (an empty title is allowed and defaults to the slug).
4. Re-open docs/modules.yaml.
Review.Expectation: The toast reads exactly: Module "clock" appended to docs/modules.yaml. Plan stub created at docs/modules/clock/project-plan.md — fill it in, then decompose it into session sets. In docs/modules.yaml: your '# my note' comment line is STILL THERE untouched, the greeter entry is byte-identical to before, and a new second entry 'slug: clock' with 'title: "clock"' (the empty title defaulted to the slug) sits at the end. docs/modules/clock/project-plan.md exists and opened in the editor.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: moduleAuthoring.test.ts > 'append preserves the existing file's text — comments and formatting included' + 'empty title defaults to the slug'; dogfood Walk A scaffolded exactly this greeter-then-clock sequence (PASS).

Review.Subarea: Walk 5 - form step 2: the planning prompt asks which module
Review.ItemLabel: 'Copy prompt for planning' shows the module QuickPick and names the module plan path
Review.HumanAction:
WHERE YOU ARE: still in Folder A. Two modules now exist (greeter, clock), so the module-aware flows will ASK which one you mean - that QuickPick is new in this release.
1. In step 2 of the form, click 'Copy prompt for planning'.
2. A QuickPick appears with placeholder 'Which module is this for?'. Click 'Greeter'.
3. Read the toast, then open a new untitled editor (Ctrl+N) and paste the clipboard (Ctrl+V).
Review.Expectation: The toast reads exactly: Plan-authoring prompt copied to clipboard. Paste it into your AI assistant, then save the result as docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md — or import it with 'Import project-plan.md'. The pasted prompt asks for a plan 'for the "Greeter" module (`greeter`) of my software project', says the plan 'covers ONLY the greeter module', and ends 'Format as a clean Markdown document I can save as docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md.'
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedActions.test.ts > 'planImport — module-aware targeting (Set 087 S3)' (copyPlanningPrompt tests); dogfood Walk D built this exact prompt (PASS).

Review.Subarea: Walk 6 - form step 3: the decomposition prompt asks which module and stamps it
Review.ItemLabel: 'Copy prompt to build session sets' shows the module QuickPick and the copied prompt is module-stamped
Review.HumanAction:
WHERE YOU ARE: still in Folder A - last walk in this folder. NOTE: you never wrote a real project plan in this folder; that is fine ON PURPOSE - the New module walks already created stub plans at docs/modules/<slug>/project-plan.md, and a stub satisfies the flow's plan-exists check.
1. In step '3. Build session sets', click 'Copy prompt to build session sets'.
2. In the 'Which module is this for?' QuickPick, the rows are 'Greeter' (with 'greeter' beside it) then 'clock', each with a 'plan: ...' detail line. Click 'clock'.
3. After the 'Session-set generation prompt copied to clipboard...' toast, paste into a new untitled editor.
4. In the pasted text, Ctrl+F for each of: module: clock — then docs/modules/clock/project-plan.md — then docs/planning/project-plan.md
Review.Expectation: The QuickPick rows appear in manifest order (Greeter first). In the pasted prompt: (a) the worked example's 'Session Set Configuration' yaml block contains the line 'module: clock' directly under the 'tier:' line; (b) a hard-requirements bullet begins '- **Module:** declare `module: clock` in EVERY generated set's'; (c) a guidance bullet begins '- **Module.** This decomposition targets the **clock** module' and contains 'Recommended (not enforced)'; (d) the closing section 'The project plan (read it from the workspace)' names docs/modules/clock/project-plan.md; (e) the search for docs/planning/project-plan.md finds NOTHING - the module's own plan replaced it.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: sessionGenPrompt.test.ts > 'buildSessionGenPrompt — module targeting (Set 087 S3)' (all three tests); consumerBootstrap.test.ts > 'module context renders the module: line'; dogfood Walk C rendered this exact prompt from the canonical bundle (PASS).

Review.Subarea: Walk 7 - Folder B: a single-module repo auto-selects with a visible notice, no picker
Review.ItemLabel: One module in the manifest: the flows never show a QuickPick but always SAY which module they targeted
Review.HumanAction:
WHERE YOU ARE: a brand-new folder. DELIBERATE SKIP: you will NOT click 'Build project structure' in this folder - that is intentional and expected. This walk proves the module features stand on their own in a bare folder; step 1 stays unchecked the whole time and that is correct.
1. Create a second EMPTY folder C:\temp\uat-single and open it in VS Code.
2. Ctrl+Shift+P > 'Dabbler: New Module' > slug: greeter > Enter > title: Greeter > Enter.
3. In the Getting Started form's step 2, click 'Copy prompt for planning'.
Review.Expectation: Step 2 works exactly as in walk 2 even though nothing was built (the manifest and stub are created the same way). Step 3 shows NO QuickPick (there is only one module). Two toasts appear: first exactly 'Using module "Greeter" (greeter) — the only module in docs/modules.yaml.' and then the 'Plan-authoring prompt copied to clipboard...' toast naming docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md as the save path.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: moduleAuthoring.test.ts > 'pickModuleForAuthoring: one module auto-selects WITH the operator notice (ruling Q2)'; gettingStartedActions.test.ts > 'copyPlanningPrompt: single module auto-targets its plan with the notice'; the flow-without-build case is exactly what the dogfood ran (scratch repo, no scaffold).

Review.Subarea: Walk 8 - Folder C: a repo with NO modules.yaml behaves exactly as before
Review.ItemLabel: No manifest: no module picker anywhere, repo-level plan path unchanged
Review.HumanAction:
WHERE YOU ARE: a brand-new folder. DELIBERATE SKIPS: do NOT click 'Build project structure' and do NOT create any module in this folder - both skips are intentional. This walk proves a repo that never opts into modules sees ZERO change from this release.
1. Create a third EMPTY folder C:\temp\uat-bare and open it in VS Code.
2. In the Getting Started form's step 2, click 'Copy prompt for planning'.
Review.Expectation: NO QuickPick and NO 'Using module...' toast appear - just the single pre-087 toast: Plan-authoring prompt copied to clipboard. Paste it into your AI assistant, then save the result as docs/planning/project-plan.md — or import it with 'Import project-plan.md'. (The pasted prompt, if you check it, names docs/planning/project-plan.md - the repo-level path, byte-identical to the previous release.)
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedActions.test.ts > 'copyPlanningPrompt copies the authoring prompt and toasts' (the Set 060 suite runs with no manifest); planImport.buildPlanningPrompt renders the repo-level prompt byte-identical (pinned by the same suite); dogfood confirmed pickModuleForAuthoring returns 'none' with zero UI on a manifest-less root.
